desc: Salesforce is a cloud-based customer relationship management (CRM) platform that helps businesses manage customer data, streamline sales, automate marketing, and provide customer service.
access_note: Developers can sign up for a free developer org, but API access for certain Professional Edition organizations may require purchase or be controlled by administrators.

desc: HubSpot is a comprehensive CRM platform offering marketing, sales, and customer service software to help businesses grow.
access_note: Developers can create a free HubSpot account and generate private app access tokens or set up OAuth 2.0 credentials via their developer dashboard.
api: REST, extensive, covering CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals, tickets), marketing, sales, and service tools, with date-based versioning.

desc: Pipedrive is an easy and effective sales CRM that drives small business growth.
access_note: API tokens can be obtained manually from the Pipedrive app, while OAuth credentials (client_id and client_secret) are managed through the Developer Hub after creating a sandbox account.

desc: Attio is a highly customizable, data-driven CRM platform that enables businesses to manage customer relationships and automate workflows.
access_note: To obtain a dedicated developer workspace, users must manually request access via live chat or email to support@attio.com, or use a free trial workspace as a sandbox.
api: REST API with broad read/write access, Webhooks, App SDK (Typescript React), and SQL query capabilities.

desc: Twenty is an open-source CRM that allows modern teams to manage customer relationships and extend its functionality through APIs, webhooks, and custom applications.
access_note: API keys can be generated from the Twenty application's Settings → API & Webhooks, and OAuth credentials are configured during app registration.
api: REST and GraphQL APIs for CRUD operations on core CRM objects and custom objects, as well as a Metadata API for schema management, offering broad and schema-per-tenant functionality.
